behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff between diff extraction and review-input construction. A unit test of only the slicer logic can miss wiring bugs where unchanged lines are reintroduced during module composition. No real network/OS or LLM dependency is required to catch this specific failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run the diff-reader and prompt-builder together on a fixture PR patch containing large unchanged sections; assert the produced review payload includes only added/modified hunks and excludes untouched file regions.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real GitHub diff-position semantics and API acceptance of line/side/commit context. Mocked clients can hide real failures because payloads that look valid locally can be rejected or misplaced in the actual PR UI.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using the automation’s computed anchors, then retrieve PR review comments via GitHub API and verify each comment lands on the intended file/hunk/line in the live diff.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about defect-detection quality from a real model output, which is non-deterministic and cannot be validated by pure wiring tests. The key failure appears when the actual LLM fails to detect the seeded bug despite correct plumbing.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real PR fixture with a seeded bug and run the production model review repeatedly under fixed prompt/settings; pass if at least one inline finding correctly references the seeded defect category and location within an acceptance threshold.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted — no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module consistency invariant between inline-comment posting results and summary assembly. A single-function unit test is too narrow if mismatch arises from state handoff/order between modules, but real external infra is not strictly required.
behaviors.test_description: Execute finding-to-inline-post and summary-generation modules together using deterministic findings and a local fake comment sink that returns posted IDs; assert summary entries are exactly the set of successfully posted inline findings (no extras).

behaviors.description: The full sequence — reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment — completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end run on a real PR and includes real model-driven review generation before posting actions. Because successful completion depends on a real LLM step plus external PR operations, lower-level tests can miss run-time failures from model output variability and downstream handling.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger the automation on a real sandbox PR branch with the production model and GitHub integration enabled; verify unattended completion with inline comments created, summary comment posted, and no manual retries/interventions required.
